message:
fix(render-term): correct right-border alignment for Unicode content

Fix the align_right_border function to properly handle multi-byte
Unicode characters when calculating padding widths:

The Problem:
- Previous implementation used .len() which returns byte count
- This caused incorrect alignment for strings containing Unicode
  characters (emojis, CJK, accented chars, etc.) since their byte
  length differs from their displayed character count

The Fix:
- Replace .len() with .chars().count() for width calculation
- Use explicit padding construction instead of format! width specifier
  which also operates on bytes, not characters
- Update assertions in tests to use character counts

This ensures ASCII art borders align correctly regardless of whether
the content contains ASCII-only or Unicode characters, fixing visual
misalignment in terminal output.

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
